methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $81M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Wispr AI is a Series A biotech and health technology company founded by Tanay Kothari and Sahaj Garg that has raised $81M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage biotech companies including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals.

Stage-progression methodology is the sole applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised ($81M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression to the next financing round yields $162M. This reflects the conventional expectation that Series A companies mature into higher-stage valuations as they de-risk and approach Series B. Peer-median analysis could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum sample thresholds (fewer than 3 datapoints). Public-comp anchoring via the sector's 6.9x median P/S multiple (across the three public tickers) was not feasible absent disclosed revenue figures for Wispr AI.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $138M (low) to $186M (high), centered on $162M. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue disclosure, which prevents revenue-multiple triangulation, and limited narrow-peer data availability in the biotech sector at this stage. The single-method foundation warrants treating this range as directional rather than definitive.
